title: Containers precpu_stats always zero
body:
### Issue Description

I am using podman on Debian (have also tested with podman desktop on macOs).  I have tested with both `runc` and `crun` as runtimes.

When trying to fetch the containers' stats with
```
curl --unix-socket /run/podman/podman.sock http://d/v5.0.0/libpod/containers/<my_container_name>/stats?stream=false
```
in the results, the `precpu_stats` are always zero.

The `precpu_stats` is the cpu statistic of last read, which is used for calculating the cpu usage percent.

Is this a bug?

```
podman version
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.3.1
API Version:  4.3.1
Go Version:   go1.19.8
Built:        Thu Jan  1 00:00:00 1970
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1.   Start an nginx container with `podman run -d -p 8080:80 --name mynginx docker.io/library/nginx`
2.  Get the container stats with `curl --unix-socket /run/podman/podman.sock http://d/v5.0.0/libpod/containers/mynginx/stats?stream=false  | jq`
3. Observe how the `precepu_stats` are always reported with zero values.


### Describe the results you received

```
 "cpu_stats": {
    "cpu_usage": {
      "total_usage": 98083000,
      "usage_in_kernelmode": 37723,
      "usage_in_usermode": 98045277
    },
    "system_cpu_usage": 77768772000,
    "cpu": 0,
    "throttling_data": {
      "periods": 0,
      "throttled_periods": 0,
      "throttled_time": 0
    }
  },
  "precpu_stats": {
    "cpu_usage": {
      "total_usage": 0,
      "usage_in_kernelmode": 0,
      "usage_in_usermode": 0
    },
    "cpu": 0,
    "throttling_data": {
      "periods": 0,
      "throttled_periods": 0,
      "throttled_time": 0
    }
  },
  "memory_stats": {
    "usage": 2498560,
    "limit": 4115369984
  },
  "name": "mynginx",
```

### Describe the results you expected

I expected the `precpu_stats` to be non zero and equal to the `cpu_stats` of last read.

### podman info output

```yaml
podman info

  arch: amd64
  buildahVersion: 1.28.2
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body: You need to stream the stats endpoint, the field is only populated on the second run AFAICT
Also can you update your system.  Your podman and podman-machine seem out of date.
Ok so you are not using the default podman machine.

> Ok so you are not using the default podman machine.

Only when I tried in a linux vm. When I tried with podman desktop in macOS, I used the default podman machine
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
